title: Prefer authored loop shapes
summary: Repeated adjacent statements that differ only by index, stride, or table slot should be checked against ordinary loop or switch forms before landing generated-looking source.
do: Try a natural loop, switch, or small helper when repeated blocks have a clear induction variable or table stride.
Keep the form backed by compiler output and local style evidence.
do_not: Preserve generated branch ladders, gotos, or copied assignment blocks without testing the normal source shape.
Rewrite working control flow only for cosmetics.
preferred_repairs: Try structured source forms first, then keep the form that validates.

title: Prefer typed fields over raw offsets
summary: Readable typed C is the target; raw pointer arithmetic and register-dump C are only temporary recovery scaffolding.
do: Use structs, arrays, enums, and helper accessors when local evidence supports the layout.
Preserve offset comments for uncertain fields and use conservative names such as field_XX or unk_XX.
do_not: Call raw pointer arithmetic or register-dump output a finished decompilation.
Infer field signedness, array dimensions, or struct names without assembly, caller, data, or header evidence.
preferred_repairs: Replace raw accesses with evidenced fields, or leave a narrow TODO when the type is not known yet.

title: Matching tactics require evidence
summary: Matching-sensitive tactics such as declaration-order changes, widened lifetimes, dummy locals, volatile locals, pragmas, local externs, and temporary padding must be narrow and evidence-backed.
do: Record the narrow build, objdiff, or checkdiff evidence that justified a tactic.
Check adjacent functions when a tactic can perturb codegen, data order, or stack shape.
do_not: Keep score-motivated codegen steering without verifier evidence.
Hide a recoverable type, signature, or control-flow issue behind a local tactic.
preferred_repairs: Try clean C first and keep tactics scoped to the function or object that proves them.

title: Avoid new pragmas and inline assembly
summary: Pragmas, register steering, asm wrappers, inline assembly, and included asm snippets are exceptions, not decompilation wins.
do: Leave real C active when it is a genuine near match unless an accepted wall policy requires otherwise.
Scope unavoidable compiler pragmas tightly and validate neighboring code.
do_not: Use asm wrappers, inline assembly, or included inc/s files to inflate match metrics.
Flip real C back to an asm wrapper except for a confirmed and logged wall.
preferred_repairs: Recover source C or document the wall in the project wall ledger before considering an exception.

title: Respect literal and data ownership
summary: Data, rodata, sdata, split, local-label, and symbol edits are matching-sensitive and should be tied to section ownership evidence.
do: Use split metadata, symbol metadata, strings, object names, and local assembly evidence before moving or naming data.
Keep data cleanup scoped to the claimed code match unless the task is explicitly data-focused.
do_not: Add dummy source storage, fake anchors, or literal externs solely to quiet a diff.
Move data across translation units without split evidence.
preferred_repairs: Fix ownership metadata when it is wrong; do not compensate with fake C storage.

title: Use evidence-backed names
summary: Names should improve reviewability without inventing meaning. Address-style names are acceptable until behavior is proven.
do: Rename only from callsites, data-table usage, resource strings, known library patterns, or assembly-confirmed behavior.
Keep old address traceability when renaming address-named functions.
do_not: Invent semantic names from one weak callsite or visual intuition.
Rename jump tables, linker labels, globals, structs, or fields without a complete reference audit.
preferred_repairs: Apply high-confidence renames globally and leave low-confidence names as notes.

title: Do not alias global renames with defines
summary: A global, extern, or data symbol should have one canonical name backed by evidence.
do: Perform evidenced renames directly across definitions, declarations, headers, callsites, and notes.
Keep one canonical declaration for each known data address.
do_not: Hide guessed names or missing declarations behind identifier-to-identifier defines.
Keep two extern declarations with different names for the same address.
preferred_repairs: Keep address-style names until a real rename is supported, then update references directly.

title: Keep headers and prototypes truthful
summary: When a body proves a signature, the owning declaration should be updated instead of hidden behind local declarations.
do: Update prototypes, typedefs, and includes where local evidence proves them.
Put public declarations in the owning header and use established include style.
do_not: Add fake source-local declarations when the real header should own them.
Leave mismatched prototypes or UNK-style signatures after recovering a body.
preferred_repairs: Fix the owning declaration instead of papering over a mismatch locally.

title: Claim matches only with verifier evidence
summary: Every claimed match or source cleanup must be backed by the narrow build, objdiff/checkdiff, or regression evidence appropriate to the blast radius.
do: Run the smallest validation that proves the touched source is safe.
Report commands that could not run and any residual risk.
do_not: Claim byte matching without verifier output.
Mix unrelated tooling, data, and decomp source edits when they can be reviewed separately.
preferred_repairs: Use runner-owned build, objdiff, checkdiff, regression, and QA artifacts as the verification ledger.

title: Validated MWCC byte-match tricks (Colosseum, GC/1.3 game TUs)
summary: Six tactics produced byte-exact matches on 2026-07-01 near-misses; try them before inventing new shapes.
A leftover '#pragma optimization_level 0' silently caps a function at low fuzzy; remove it before any other work.
Multi-word copies match as one struct/u64 assignment (forward lwz/lwz/stw/stw), not per-word locals.
When target narrows args at the callsite (clrlwi before bl), widen the function's own params and narrow the CALLEE's local extern prototype instead of narrowing at entry.
An extra unused parameter (matching target's untouched arg register) shifts scratch-register assignment; also fix the callee prototype it propagates to.
Zeroing/init loops over stride tables match with struct-typed array indexing (((Entry*)tbl)[i].field=0), not pointer+multiply arithmetic.
64-bit flag words must be a true u64 field with u64 mask constants; u32-pair decomposition changes instruction shape (rlwinm. instead of and/or.) and regresses.

To get a u64 constant into the PPC register-pair ABI slots (hi r5 / lo r6), pass it as a genuine u64 FUNCTION PARAMETER of a static (auto-inlined) helper — macro-substituted u64 literals never produce that allocation (fixed 8/11 residual mismatches per function in the mcmd family, commit 8a29b94f).

If a unit's flags lack -O4,s (check configure.py; e.g. fight_range_80211A00 vs pokemon.c), a per-function '#pragma optimize_for_size on' reproduces the -O4,s codegen (defeats loop unrolls, restores stmw prologues, short branchless idioms) — fixed 2 fns in wave 1 (commit f97eb7c0).

Tri-state gate epilogues: 'if (r==0) return 0; if (r==-1) return 1; return 1;' reproduces MWCC's genuinely dead 'cmpwi r,-1' instruction — keep the dead compare, do not simplify. Also: comparison operand order (max < ary[i] vs ary[i] > max) selects the emitted cmpw operand order.

Guard-clause polarity flip: when the diff shows the default-value assignment on the opposite branch-fusion path, swap if/else polarity (if(cond){A}else{B} -> if(!cond){B}else{A}) — works for flag=0/1 set patterns (2 confirmed), NOT for bare early-return guards.

Immediate forms matter: 'flags &= 0xbf' vs 'flags &= ~0x40' select different instructions; try the explicit-constant form. Declaration order of locals is a real register-assignment lever (fn_800AB4FC).

DATA MATCHING recipe (validated, matched_data 12364->12684): data units are declspec-section '.sdata2'/.rodata range files, const globals in exact address order, names+SIZES from symbols.txt (a [7] string must be [7] even if padding follows — symbol size mismatch blocks crediting), force_active pragma for dead padding symbols; crediting is all-or-nothing per unit section at 100% exact.
Shared-return blocks need explicit 'goto sharedLabel;' — MWCC will not merge multiple 'if(x) return N;' guards into one return block on its own (took a fn from 88.75% to 100%).

'lis rX,0x1 / subi rX,rX,1' encodes constant 0xFFFF (li cannot load it unsigned). Stack-buffer over-sizing shows as a frame-size mismatch (e.g. -0x60 vs -0x50) — shrink local arrays to what the frame demands.
Raw auto-generated stubs frequently mistranslate 'clrlwi. / bne|beq' zero-tests as '== 1' compares — re-derive branch polarity from the asm, never trust stub comparisons.
do: Grep the function region for '#pragma optimization_level' before diagnosing a low fuzzy score.
Read the objdiff instruction diff first and classify the gap: opcode-shape difference (fixable in C) vs pure register-letter/scheduling difference (see canonicalization-walls standard).
For vendor/library strata (MusyX, Dolphin SDK, HSD, TRK, libm), fetch the public matched reference source and transcribe, adapting to target asm; do not reinvent bodies.

Verify wins against the authoritative diff pairing only: objdiff-cli diff -p . -u <unit> (objdiff.json pairs obj/ vs the dtk-extracted target). NEVER hand-compare against build/GC6E01/base/*.o — stale copies there produced 5 false-positive 'wins' in one session. Note the JSON diff's left/right orientation by checking which side has gap_* symbols (that side is the extracted TARGET).
do_not: Do not add asm wrappers or activate .inc includes to fake progress.
Do not keep a tactic that did not move the diff; revert to the simplest matching-shape candidate.
Never remove an existing '#pragma peephole off' from a decently-matching function — observed 93.6% -> 23.6% collapse; only ADD pragmas experimentally.
evidence_refs: pkmn-colosseum commit b7c8ba19 (13 byte-exact matches, people_field unit 83->94)

title: Recognize compiler-canonicalization walls and stop
summary: Some 95-99% near-misses are MWCC-internal canonicalization artifacts that NO semantically-equivalent C rewrite can move; recognize them and stop instead of burning attempts.

Wall signatures (verified 2026-07-01): cyclic permutation of callee-saved locals (r27-r31 rotation); within-schedule ordering of two independent constant materializations. Register-letter-swap diffs CAN also be per-TU compiler-version artifacts — a mw_version sweep flipped dataGetCurve/dataGetKeymap to 100% under GC/1.3.2.

Verified-useless levers for these walls: declaration order, statement order, operand order, register keyword (MWCC ignores it here), '#pragma scheduling off' (always regresses), temp-variable hoisting, u32 decomposition of u64 ops.

NEW confirmed wall class: single-statement guards compiling to bne+b (2 insns) vs target's beq (1 insn) in units WITHOUT -O4,s — unfixable by &&-combining, pragmas, or temp vars (0/6+ fns across gs_field_world). Park on sight of this exact branch-shape diff.
do: Classify the residual diff from objdiff: if every instruction opcode+operand-shape matches and only register letters or adjacent-instruction order differ, mark the target as a canonicalization wall.

Before declaring a wall, check version-sensitivity: if the diff is identical across all MWCC versions (like the 11-fn mcmd family, invariant at 97.54%), it is a shared SOURCE-level pattern — re-derive the C shape; if it moves with version/flags, report it for a per-TU mw_version/flags experiment.
Bank the near-miss as a CodeCandidate partial before moving on.

mcmd-family status after shape re-derivation: 99.62% uniform; sole residual is a 3-insn scratch-register-bank artifact in the bool-ify idiom (extrwi/neg/or) — ~35 variants exhausted; treat as a true wall unless new levers appear.
do_not: Do not spend more than ~3 source-level attempts on a diff already classified as a pure coloring/scheduling artifact.
Do not switch to stronger models for these targets; model capability does not change compiler canonicalization.
evidence_refs: pkmn-colosseum mw_version sweep 2026-07-01: GC/1.3.2 flipped dataGetCurve/dataGetKeymap (commit b4d56971); mcmd family version-INVARIANT at 97.54% = source-level; -use_lmw_stmw on trades 2 wins for 2 losses
escalation telemetry 2026-07-01: 0 above-rung-0 cracks from opus/fable on flag-class walls

title: Scaffold duplicates of header static inlines: delete, don't redefine
body: When a static inline helper in a shared header (hsd_object.h/hsd_class.h style) also appears as an address-scaffolded standalone function in a TU's range, do NOT keep a second definition. The header's inline is the real source; the standalone address in the target binary is the linker folding that TU's out-of-lined instance. Delete the scaffold duplicate and let the header supply it; pair the emitted out-of-line copy via the name_ADDR local-symbol convention if needed. Same scaffold-reconciliation class: hand-written placeholder + later #if 0/#else scaffold for the SAME symbol in one file — keep the disassembly-derived scaffold body, delete or forward-declare the placeholder. Known parked site: gs_field_resource.c floorRead*PreFunc family, where the in-file string-anchored attribution disagrees with XD structural verdicts — reconcile in-file evidence first, it is usually deeper.

title: Handler registration tables = dispatch-table naming proof
body: When a family of callbacks exists (floorRead*, WazaHit*, menu handlers), hunt for the registration/dispatch TABLE in .data: fixed-stride entries containing function pointers. Decode it straight from the DOL (struct-unpack the data section) and the table pairs/orders the handlers authoritatively — stronger than strings or XD structural ports. Proven on the floorRead family: lbl_8036C2A0 (16-byte entries: size, type-id, PreFunc, PostFunc) named 13 fns in one pass and OVERTURNED a string-only assignment (both particle PreFuncs reference the same string; only the table disambiguates). Method: take 2-3 known members, grep their addresses as big-endian bytes in the DOL data sections, infer entry stride/layout from the hit spacing, then decode the whole table. Same technique should crack the WazaHitNNN numeric suffixes (find the move-effect dispatch table).

title: Address-order anchoring for numbered template families
body: For a numbered handler family (WazaHitNNN etc.) where per-body evidence cannot pin the number: collect ALL family members (e.g. distinct pointers from a dispatch/per-item table via tools/dolread.py findptr/table), sort by address, and check the named anchors — if address order reproduces ID order with zero violations (Colosseum WazaHit: descending address = ascending NNN across 33 anchors), then any gap where two anchors' rank distance equals their ID distance forces every intermediate assignment exactly. Yielded 83 exact WazaHitNNN names in one pass. Corroborate with XD sizes, but beware the delegate-inlining pattern: XD implements trivial variants as 0x20 stubs (bl familyDefault); Colosseum inlines the default body (0xB0) — size mismatch in that shape CONFIRMS rather than refutes. Re-derive independently before applying: the fill is a pure function of (table, symbols), ~30 lines of python. SCOPE CAVEAT (2026-07-02): the heuristic holds for the WazaHit family but FAILS inside the scrCmd field-script opcode table (lbl_802E1CF0) — camera family idx43-47 is address-ordered 47,45,44,43,46. Validate per-table against ALL anchors (zero violations) before trusting any fill; if anchors violate order, fall back to string/wrapper evidence only. Also: anchor the decode at the object's TRUE base found via its bound-checked consumer, not at the first pointer-looking word — the fade table's fnPtr-LAST field order silently mis-pairs otherwise.
